No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course is a Master's level legal program, directly contributing to quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and developing professional skills for employment (Target 4.4).

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops professional skills for practice (Core Competency A) and includes corporate talks ('企業座談'), directly supporting productive employment and decent work (Target 8.5).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to industry, innovation, or infrastructure.

Reduced Inequalities.reason: While legal education can indirectly impact inequalities, the syllabus provides no explicit focus on this SDG.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The mention of 'Respect Copyright' relates to intellectual property, not directly to sustainable consumption, production patterns, or corporate sustainability reporting (Target 12.6).

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus connects to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: As a U.S. Legal Research, Analysis & Writing course, it fundamentally promotes the rule of law (Target 16.3) and trains professionals essential for developing effective, accountable, and transparent institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course fosters a 'global vision' (Core Competency D), aligning with international cooperation and knowledge sharing (Target 17.6). '企業座談' (corporate talks) also represent a form of multi-stakeholder partnership (Target 17.17).
